walkthrough.title: Prepare the Market Deck
walkthrough.text: Let's set up Hero Realms! Shuffle all eighty market cards into one face-down deck. These cards contain actions and champions from four factions: Imperial, Necros, Wild, and Guild. Place this deck in the center of the table where everyone can reach it.

walkthrough.title: Reveal the Market Row
walkthrough.text: Flip the top five cards from the market deck face up in a row. This is the Market Row, which is the shared shop where everyone buys cards during the game. Whenever someone buys a card from the row, immediately replace it with the next card from the market deck.

walkthrough.title: Set Out Fire Gems
walkthrough.text: Place the ten Fire Gem cards in a face-up pile next to the Market Row. Fire Gems are always available to purchase for two gold. They provide two gold when played, or you can sacrifice them for three combat damage. They are a solid economy upgrade.

walkthrough.title: Build Starting Decks
walkthrough.text: Each player takes a starting deck of ten cards: seven Gold cards, one Dagger card, one Ruby card, and one Short Sword card. Shuffle your ten cards and place them face down as your personal draw deck. This starting deck is weak on purpose. You will improve it by buying better cards.

walkthrough.title: Set Authority and Draw
walkthrough.text: Each player sets their Authority to fifty. This is your health total. Draw three cards for your opening hand. From your second turn onward, you will draw five cards at the end of each turn. Choose a starting player randomly and begin!

summary.title: Set Authority and Draw

Opening hand: 3 cards (5 cards from turn 2 onward)
Choose starting player randomly

walkthrough.title: Turn Overview
walkthrough.text: Your turn has three phases. First, the Main Phase where you play cards, buy new ones, and attack. Second, the Discard Phase where you discard all played cards and your remaining hand. Third, the Draw Phase where you draw five new cards. Let's break down the Main Phase since that is where everything happens.

walkthrough.title: Playing Cards
walkthrough.text: During your Main Phase, play any number of cards from your hand in any order. Action cards provide immediate effects like Gold, Combat, or card draw, and then go to your discard pile at end of turn. Champions are placed in front of you and stay in play between turns until they are stunned.

walkthrough.title: Accumulating Resources
walkthrough.text: Gold and Combat pool up during your turn. As you play cards that generate Gold, it all adds together. Same with Combat. You can spend Gold to buy cards and Combat to attack at any point during your Main Phase. Unspent Gold and Combat disappear at the end of your turn, so use them or lose them.

walkthrough.title: Buying Cards
walkthrough.text: Spend Gold to buy cards from the Market Row or Fire Gem pile. Each card shows its cost in the upper right corner. Purchased cards go directly to your discard pile, not your hand. They will show up when you shuffle your discard pile into a new draw deck. Replace purchased Market Row cards immediately.

walkthrough.title: Attacking
walkthrough.text: Spend Combat to reduce your opponent's Authority. But there is a catch. If your opponent has any Guard champions in play, you must stun all Guards before you can attack the player directly. To stun a champion, spend Combat equal to its defense value. Stunned champions go to their owner's discard pile.

walkthrough.title: Champions and Abilities
walkthrough.text: Champions stay in front of you between turns. At the start of your turn, ready all your champions by turning them upright. Each champion may have an Expend ability that you activate by turning it sideways, an Ally ability that triggers when another card of the same faction is in play, and a Sacrifice ability that removes the card permanently for a powerful effect.

walkthrough.title: Faction Ally Abilities
walkthrough.text: Many cards have Ally abilities that trigger when another card of the same faction is in play. Champions in play from previous turns count for this. Building around one or two factions ensures you trigger Ally abilities consistently. A card without its Ally bonus is noticeably weaker than one with it.

walkthrough.title: Sacrifice Mechanic
walkthrough.text: Some cards have a Sacrifice ability. When you sacrifice a card, remove it from the game permanently and gain a powerful one-time effect. This is great for two purposes. First, it gives you explosive plays at critical moments. Second, sacrificing weak starting cards like Gold and Dagger thins your deck so you draw your good cards more often.

walkthrough.title: Drawing and Shuffling
walkthrough.text: At the end of your turn, discard everything and draw five new cards. If your draw deck runs out, shuffle your discard pile to form a new draw deck. This means every card you buy will eventually cycle into your hand. The game continues until one player's Authority reaches zero.

walkthrough.title: Winning the Game
walkthrough.text: The game ends immediately when a player's Authority drops to zero or below. The last player standing wins. In a three or four player game, eliminated players are out and play continues until only one remains. There is no point scoring. Pure elimination.

Draw 5 cards at end of turn

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Hero Realms
walkthrough.text: We will play through several turns of a two-player game, showing you how to buy cards, build your deck, attack your opponent, and avoid common beginner mistakes. Both players start with fifty Authority and three-card opening hands. Let's go!

walkthrough.title: Player 1's Opening Turn
walkthrough.text: Player 1 draws their three starting cards: two Gold cards and a Dagger. That gives them two Gold to spend and one Combat. Player 1 looks at the Market Row and buys a two-cost Guild champion that generates two Gold when expended. The one Combat goes toward the opponent's Authority, dropping it to forty-nine.

walkthrough.title: Player 2's Opening Turn
walkthrough.text: Player 2 draws two Gold cards and a Ruby. The Ruby gives two Gold, so Player 2 has four Gold total. That is enough to buy a nice mid-range card from the Market Row. Player 2 picks up a Wild faction action card that deals five Combat. A strong early purchase!

walkthrough.title: Building Economy
walkthrough.text: For the next few turns, both players focus on buying cards that generate Gold. Economy is king in the early game. The more Gold you generate per hand, the better cards you can afford. Fire Gems are a solid reliable purchase at two Gold each, giving you two Gold when played or three Combat via sacrifice.

walkthrough.title: Player 1's Mistake: Faction Sprawl
walkthrough.text: Player 1 has been buying whichever card looks strongest from the Market Row regardless of faction. After several turns, they have cards from all four factions: Imperial, Necros, Wild, and Guild. None of their Ally abilities trigger because they rarely have two cards from the same faction in the same hand.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Focus on One or Two Factions
walkthrough.text: Ally abilities are a major source of power in Hero Realms. They only trigger when you have another card of the same faction in play. If you buy from all four factions, you rarely have faction pairs. Focus your purchases on one or two factions so that most hands trigger multiple Ally bonuses.

walkthrough.title: Player 2 Deploys a Guard Champion
walkthrough.text: Player 2 plays an Imperial Guard champion with four defense. This champion must be stunned before Player 1 can deal Combat damage directly to Player 2. Player 1 now needs to generate enough Combat to take down the Guard before any leftover Combat can hit Player 2's Authority.

walkthrough.title: Player 1's Mistake: Ignoring the Guard
walkthrough.text: Player 1 does not understand the Guard mechanic and tries to attack Player 2 directly. But the rules say you must stun all Guard champions first. Player 1 spends six Combat trying to hit Player 2, but it all goes toward stunning the Guard instead. Player 1 thought they dealt six damage to Player 2, but actually they only stunned the Guard and dealt zero direct damage.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Always Stun Guards First
walkthrough.text: Guard champions are shields. You cannot bypass them. All Combat must go toward stunning Guards before any can hit the player. When facing Guards, calculate how much Combat you need to remove them and how much will be left over for direct damage. Sometimes it is better to build up for a big turn that clears Guards and deals real damage.

walkthrough.title: Deck Thinning in Action
walkthrough.text: Player 2 sacrifices a weak starting Gold card to use its sacrifice ability. That card is removed from the game permanently. Now Player 2 has nine cards in their deck instead of ten, plus several purchased upgrades. The ratio of good cards to bad cards improves every time you thin out a weak starter.

walkthrough.title: The Mid-Game Push
walkthrough.text: Both players now have upgraded decks. Player 2 triggers two Ally abilities in one turn because they focused on Guild cards. Their turn generates eight Gold and seven Combat. They buy an expensive champion and deal seven damage to Player 1. Focused faction building is paying off.

walkthrough.title: Player 1's Mistake: Wasting Combat
walkthrough.text: Player 1 generates four Combat but does not spend it because there is no Guard to stun and they forgot they can attack the player directly. All four Combat vanishes at the end of their turn. Unused Combat is completely wasted. Always spend your Combat on something, even if it is just chipping away at the opponent's Authority.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Never Waste Combat
walkthrough.text: Combat disappears at the end of your turn. Always spend it. Even one point of damage to an opponent's Authority adds up over the course of the game. If you cannot stun a champion, hit the player directly. If Guards are in the way, save up for a bigger turn or find ways to generate more Combat this turn.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: You have seen how to build a deck, use champions, trigger Ally abilities, and attack through Guards. Remember: focus on one or two factions for Ally triggers, always stun Guards before attacking players, and never waste Combat. You are ready for a full game!

walkthrough.title: Before You Battle
walkthrough.text:
Before you start a full game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategy basics and common mistakes to avoid.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to factions, card types, and keywords.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

summary.title: Before You Battle
bullets: General Tips → strategy basics
Appendix → factions, card types, keywords
GM Guide → Q&A for answers

walkthrough.title: Economy First
walkthrough.text: In the first few turns, prioritize buying cards that generate Gold. You need economy to afford the powerful five, six, and seven cost cards that win games. Fire Gems and Guild cards are great early economy picks. Do not rush to buy expensive Combat cards before you can reliably afford them.

walkthrough.title: Focus Your Factions
walkthrough.text: Pick one or two factions and buy almost exclusively from them. Ally abilities are one of the biggest power boosts in the game, and they only trigger when you have same-faction cards together. A focused deck with reliable triggers will outperform a scattered collection of individually strong cards.

walkthrough.title: Champions Are High Priority
walkthrough.text: Champions stay in play between turns, providing value every round through their Expend abilities. Guard champions protect your Authority. Non-Guard champions generate resources or deal damage repeatedly. Buy champions early and play them often. A champion that survives three turns has tripled its value.

walkthrough.title: Thin Your Deck
walkthrough.text: Your starting cards are weak. Every time you sacrifice a Gold or Dagger card, your deck gets stronger because you draw your purchased upgrades more often. Look for cards with sacrifice abilities that let you remove starting cards. A lean eight-card deck full of strong cards beats a bloated fifteen-card deck with junk.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Three mistakes cost beginners the most games. First, buying from all four factions and never triggering Ally abilities. Second, leaving Guard champions alive turn after turn instead of prioritizing their removal. Third, forgetting to spend Combat at the end of your turn, letting damage vanish into nothing.

walkthrough.title: Stun Enemy Champions
walkthrough.text: An opponent's champion that survives generates value every turn. Prioritize stunning dangerous champions, especially those with strong Expend abilities or Guard. Sometimes spending all your Combat on a champion instead of chipping at Authority is the right play. A dead champion cannot generate more value.

walkthrough.title: Know When to Pivot
walkthrough.text: If your opponent is building faster than you, shift from economy to aggression. If they are low on Authority, buy Combat-heavy cards and close out the game. If you are behind, invest in Guard champions and healing to buy time. Flexibility wins games in Hero Realms.

walkthrough.title: Faction Deep Dives
walkthrough.text: Each faction has a distinct playstyle. Imperial focuses on Authority gain and card draw, creating sustain. Necros uses sacrifice and discard effects to control the game. Wild delivers massive Combat damage for aggressive finishes. Guild generates trade economy for expensive cards. Choose your faction based on what the Market Row offers early.

walkthrough.title: Explosive Turn Setup
walkthrough.text: The best turns in Hero Realms chain multiple Ally abilities and card draw effects together. If you have three Guild cards and two draw effects in one hand, you might play eight or nine cards in a single turn. Build your deck around these explosive combo turns rather than consistent moderate turns.

walkthrough.title: Fire Gem Sacrifice Timing
walkthrough.text: Fire Gems provide two Gold normally but can be sacrificed for three Combat. Save the sacrifice for a killing blow or to stun a critical Guard champion. Once sacrificed, the Fire Gem is gone forever, so make sure the three Combat matters more than the ongoing two Gold per shuffle.

walkthrough.title: Guard Wall Strategy
walkthrough.text: Deploying multiple Guard champions creates a wall that opponents must break through before dealing any direct damage. This buys you time to build your deck. Imperial and Guild factions have strong Guard options. A wall of two or three Guards can absorb ten or more Combat, protecting you through critical deck-building turns.

walkthrough.title: Market Row Manipulation
walkthrough.text: When the Market Row has cards that would strongly benefit your opponent, consider buying them even if they are not ideal for you. This is called hate-drafting. Alternatively, if the Market Row is full of cards in your opponent's faction, buying any card clears a slot and might reveal something better for you.

walkthrough.title: Aggression Timing
walkthrough.text: There is a tipping point in every game where you should stop buying economy cards and start maximizing damage. This usually happens when your opponent drops below twenty Authority. At that point, every Combat matters more than Gold. Shift your purchases to direct damage cards and sacrifice Fire Gems for burst Combat.

walkthrough.title: Deck Cycling Speed
walkthrough.text: A smaller deck cycles faster, meaning you see your best cards more often. Sacrifice weak cards aggressively. Buy cards that draw additional cards to burn through your deck faster. The player whose power cards show up every three turns will outperform the player who sees them every five turns.

summary.title: Faction Deep Dives
bullets: Imperial: sustain and draw
Necros: sacrifice and control
Wild: massive Combat
Guild: economy for expensive cards

entries.definition: Your health total, starting at fifty. When it reaches zero, you are eliminated. The last player standing wins.
Currency used to buy cards from the Market Row and Fire Gem pile. Pools during your turn and vanishes at end of turn.
Damage resource used to attack opponents and stun champions. Pools during your turn and vanishes at end of turn.
Five face-up cards available for purchase. Refilled immediately when a card is bought.
Always-available card costing two Gold. Provides two Gold when played or can be sacrificed for three Combat.
A card played for immediate effect during your Main Phase. Discarded at end of turn.
A card that stays in play between turns. Has defense value. Can be stunned by spending Combat equal to its defense.
A champion keyword. All Guard champions must be stunned before you can attack the player or non-Guard champions.
Champion ability activated by turning the card sideways. Can only be used once per turn. Champion readies at start of your next turn.
Bonus ability that triggers when another card of the same faction is in play. Champions from previous turns count.
Remove the card from the game permanently for a powerful one-time effect. Also used to thin weak cards from your deck.
Faction focused on Authority gain, card draw, and healing. Creates sustain and card advantage.
Faction focused on sacrifice effects, discard manipulation, and graveyard interaction. Control-oriented.
Faction focused on high Combat damage and direct aggression. Best for rushing opponents down.
Faction focused on Gold generation and versatile economy. Enables purchasing expensive power cards.
Spending Combat equal to a champion's defense to defeat it. Stunned champions go to owner's discard pile at start of their next turn.
Removing weak cards from your deck via sacrifice to improve the average quality of your draws.
At the start of your turn, ready all your champions (turn upright) and discard any stunned champions.
